_meta.description: Specialization rules applied on top of base_program.json by the generator. Picking a muscle ADDS direct sets (is_specialization=true) and REDUCES sets elsewhere to protect total recovery. weekly_cap_sets is the MRV-informed ceiling; the generator must refuse to push a muscle's total weekly direct sets above this.

volume_landmarks_reference._comment: Weekly DIRECT working sets per muscle, consensus RP/Israetel + Schoenfeld meta-analyses. [MEV, MAV-range, MRV]. Used to set add amounts and caps. Side delts and biceps tolerate high volume; quads/hams have lower ceilings due to systemic fatigue.

_meta.reassess_prompt: After the block: keep this muscle, switch to another, or return to balanced? Lagging muscles benefit from repeating; if it has caught up, rotate.
_meta.generator_contract: Idempotent. base -> specialize -> reset -> re-specialize must never duplicate slots or exceed weekly_cap_sets. 'add' inserts new slots (is_specialization=true) on the named day. 'reduce' lowers an existing slot's set count to sets_to (never below 1; if the slot is gone, skip). 'day' refers to day_order in base_program.

biceps.rationale: Biceps get ~3 direct sets in base + indirect from all pulling. Specializing pushes direct work to ~10/week across both upper days. Trim chest-supported row (back already gets heavy pulldown + pull-up + barbell row) to protect recovery and elbow load.

triceps.rationale: Triceps get ~3 direct + heavy indirect from all pressing. Add overhead (long-head stretch) on Day 1 and skull crusher on Day 3, reaching ~9 direct/week. Trim a lateral-raise set on the heavier day; side delts are already well served.

chest.rationale: Base chest is ~9 direct. Add a fly (isolation, stretch-biased) on Day 1 and an extra incline/flat press emphasis on Day 3, reaching ~14-15/week. Trim one back row set since pull volume is high and we want recovery budget on chest.

back.rationale: Base back is ~9 direct. Add a single-arm row on Day 1 and a straight-arm pulldown (lat isolation) on Day 3, reaching ~14/week. Trim one chest pressing set on Day 3 to balance fatigue.

shoulders_side.rationale: Side delts tolerate very high volume and barely add systemic fatigue. Base is ~7. Add cable laterals both days for constant-tension volume, reaching ~16-18/week with minimal recovery cost — no reduction needed.

shoulders_rear.rationale: Rear delts are usually underdone and recover fast. Base is ~3 (one fly on Day 3). Add reverse pec deck on Day 1 and face pulls on Day 3, reaching ~11-12/week. No reduction; low fatigue cost.

quads.rationale: Legs are 1x/week in the base (upper-priority). Quad specialization adds a second stimulus: hack squat + leg extension on the lower day, and a unilateral quad move can be added on Day 1 as accessory. Quads fatigue systemically, so cap is conservative.

hamstrings.rationale: Base hams ~6 (RDL + seated curl). Add a lying leg curl on the lower day for a second curl angle, reaching ~9-10/week. RDL already covers the hip-hinge stretch component.

calves.rationale: Base calves ~3. Notoriously stubborn; respond to frequency and full-stretch reps. Add seated calf raise (soleus bias) on the lower day and standing on an upper day for 2x frequency, reaching ~9/week.

abs.rationale: Base abs ~3 (hanging leg raise on lower day). Add cable crunch (loaded spinal flexion) on each upper day for frequency, reaching ~9/week. Low systemic cost.

forearms.rationale: Not in the volume-landmark core table (grows from grip work during all pulling). For users who want explicit forearm size, add wrist curls + reverse wrist curls on Day 3. High reps. No reduction.
